projects.name: BitcoinFriendlySA
projects.description: BitcoinFriendlySA is a platform (a local directory and content hub) that helps South Africans spend and accept Bitcoin with confidence.
projects.initiatives: Creating awareness around spending and accepting Bitcoin in South Africa via the Academy. My goal is to continue creating content on this topic and become the number one resource people turn to when they want to learn how this stuff actually works.
projects.challenges: 1. Keeping the content accurate in an ecosystem that's constantly changing. A good example of this is the Editor's note I had to include here: https://www.bitcoinfriendlysa.co.za/academy/31-top-places-to-spend-your-crypto-in-south-africa-2025. 2. Educating both the spenders and merchants who are curious about accepting Bitcoin. 3. Driving real-world spending when many still see BTC as a SoV. 4. Making some money off what I do right now to keep things ticking along. 5. Deciding where to focus my attention. I need to prioritise what content is NB and can't execute every idea that pops into my head.

projects.name: BITCOIN AVES
projects.description:
"Bitcoin Aves is a grassroots Bitcoin initiative based in Akatsi, Ghana. We focus on building a sustainable Circular Economy, teaching Bitcoin in a way that connects with local culture and needs. Our goal is to drive grassroots adoption through clear, accessible learning. At the heart of it all are values like decentralization, financial freedom, and sovereignty.
Rooted in the belief that true empowerment begins with understanding, Bitcoin Aves simplifies the complex, challenges the status quo, and champions economic independence, starting from Akatsi and rippling outward."

projects.name: MulaStack
projects.description: MulaStack is Kenya's first Bitcoin on-ramp and off-ramp solution, designed to make saving, investing and transacting in Bitcoin effortless across Africa. We address the growing demand for Bitcoin as a reliable way to diversify portfolios and preserve wealth. Our platform enables users to seamlessly convert KES into Bitcoin via Mpesa, making it easy to save, invest and withdraw Bitcoin using just a mobile phone. MulaStack bridges the gap between local currencies and Bitcoin, empowering Africans to participate in the global Bitcoin economy without barriers.

projects.name: What's Haute With Halima
projects.description: "What's Haute With Halima" is a dynamic Bitcoin and lifestyle podcast that breaks down the future of money in a simple, engaging, and culturally relevant way. Hosted by Halima, the show blends clear Bitcoin education, real-life storytelling, and everyday lifestyle conversations to help listeners understand how Bitcoin fits into their lives, whether they're beginners or already curious about the Bitcoin space.
projects.initiatives: My current project is "What's Haute With Halima," a Bitcoin-focused podcast where I invite Bitcoin founders, builders, and innovators to share their stories. The goal is to inspire, educate, and make Bitcoin more relatable by highlighting real journeys, challenges, and successes within the ecosystem. Through these conversations — combined with my own storytelling and lifestyle-based explanations — I simplify Bitcoin for everyday people and help them see how it connects to their lives.
projects.challenges: One of my biggest challenges is access to resources and mentorship within the global Bitcoin ecosystem, especially as I work to grow a platform that educates and inspires people at scale. I often have to navigate technical topics on my own while ensuring the content remains accurate, relatable, and accessible. Another challenge is reaching a wider audience beyond my immediate community. Although there is strong interest in Bitcoin, many people still lack foundational understanding, so I constantly need to simplify complex ideas without losing depth. I also face the challenge of balancing multiple initiatives — hosting the podcast, producing educational series, contributing to Bitcoin translations, and managing my creative businesses — all while maintaining consistency and high-quality output.

projects.name: BitcoinBabies
projects.description: We are a non-profit organization that aims to empower mothers around the world. We take a holistic approach where we address financial empowerment through Bitcoin education, infant nutrition, and most importantly, postpartum mental wellness.
projects.initiatives: focuses on empowering mothers by providing them with Bitcoin stipends, financial literacy, and entrepreneurial skills.
projects.impact: Currently, we're deeply engaged in the Bitcoin Babies program, where we're closely monitoring 12 mothers over the span of a year. We provide weekly payouts of satoshis to enhance the well-being of both the mothers and their babies. As part of this program, we educate the mothers on topics such as entrepreneurship, financial health, and Bitcoin, and we also offer courses like cooking classes. Additionally, we're actively working to onboard merchants in the mothers' communities, enabling them to spend their satoshis locally.
projects.challenges: Getting merchants onboard who are willing to accept Bitcoin payments.

projects.name: BitDevs Zambia
projects.description: BitDevs Zambia is a community of Bitcoin developers, educators, builders, and technically curious enthusiasts based in Zambia. It explores the inner workings of Bitcoin through meetups, workshops, bootcamps, study groups, and Socratic Seminars.

projects.name: Orphans of Uganda Children Center
projects.description: Orphans of 🇺🇬Uganda Children Center, is a none profit/religious organizations taking care of 77 orphaned children. The Orphans of Uganda Children Center cares for 77 vulnerable orphans, providing them with food, shelter, education, and emotional support. In a country with limited resources and few funding options, the center sustains itself through Bitcoin donations, which help cover essential expenses. This innovative use of cryptocurrency allows the center to receive direct support from donors worldwide, ensuring these children have a brighter future despite challenging circumstances.

We bridge the gap between Bitcoin and local banking systems by enabling instant bank transfers, fast on- and off-ramps, and seamless payments without the complexity of traditional exchanges.

projects.name: Bitcoin Campus Zambia
projects.description: Bitcoin Campus Zambia is a student-led initiative focused on empowering youths and students in Zambia through Bitcoin education. We aim to build a knowledgeable and financially independent generation by teaching young people how Bitcoin works, how to earn and use sats responsibly, and how to secure their funds through self-custody. Our mission is to bring financial freedom to Zambia, starting with Lusaka's youth.
projects.initiatives: Inflation Simulator - Demonstrating the effects of inflation and how Bitcoin can serve as a hedge. Bitcoin Manifesto - Promoting Bitcoin adoption and awareness across African campuses. Starter Book - A beginner-friendly Bitcoin guide designed for students and young learners. Bitcoin Mining Education - Teaching students about mining and the technology behind Bitcoin. Bitcoin Test - Interactive tools and quizzes to help learners test their Bitcoin knowledge. Regular Campus Meetups - Hosting events and discussions to onboard new users and build a strong Bitcoin youth community.
projects.challenges: Our biggest challenge is funding. Bitcoin Campus Zambia operates as a student-led and volunteer-driven initiative, and we currently rely on small donations from supporters. Since we offer all our programs, materials, and meetups completely free to students, this model is not sustainable long-term. We need consistent funding to expand our reach, develop better educational resources, and organize more campus events.

projects.name: BitSpenda
projects.description: BitSpenda is a Bitcoin-powered payment platform that makes it easy for anyone to convert Bitcoin into mobile money and spend it locally. We're focused on bridging the gap between Bitcoin and everyday use in Africa, starting with fast, simple transactions that help people actually use Bitcoin in real life.

projects.name: Pay With Flash
projects.description: Pay With Flash lets African merchants accept Bitcoin payments with instant settlement into local currency or stable value. Our platform removes the complexity of crypto payments—no price risk, no custody headaches, no technical setup. Whether online or in-store, Pay With Flash makes Bitcoin a fast, modern, and practical payment option for everyday commerce.
projects.initiatives: Pay With Flash is building the payment infrastructure that turns Bitcoin into a practical, everyday payment method for African commerce. We enable merchants across retail, hospitality, e-commerce, and services to accept Bitcoin instantly with automatic settlement into local currency or stable value, removing volatility and operational risk. Through seamless APIs, hosted checkout, and in-store QR/POS tools, Pay With Flash makes Bitcoin acceptance as easy as mobile money or card payments—unlocking access to global crypto-native customers for local businesses.

projects.name: Bitsavers Eduhub

Through a curriculum rooted in Bitcoin education and decentralized saving practices like Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA), Bitsavers empowers students and recent graduates to overcome inflation, embrace financial sovereignty, and make informed financial choices.

projects.name: West Data Group
projects.description: West Data Group (WDG) is a Hong Kong-based company under United International Engineering, specializing in the investment and construction of data centers. With a focus on sectors such as telecommunications, cryptocurrency mining, and AI training, WDG has developed strong partnerships with global leaders like HUAWEI, ZTE, and Canaan Inc., as well as key organizations in Ethiopia, including Ethio Telecom and Ethiopia Electric Power. In 2023, WDG expanded into Ethiopia, establishing Wes Data Center Service PLC and launching a significant 30MW data center project, positioning itself as a leader in data processing and digitalization efforts.

projects.description: Bitcoin Nairobi is a Bitcoin Circular Economy in Nairobi, Kenya. Our mission is to empower Bitcoin Education through Bitcoin School Kenya to underserved community in Kenya and Communities and also to drive Bitcoin Adoption through onboarding merchants to accept Bitcoin after also through helping communities build Bitcoin Circular Economies such communities are like Afribit Kibera and Bitcoin Mombasa. We also partner with Bitcoin Tech companies like Btrust Fedimint and TBD to train our students on Bitcoin development and to build bitcoin based solutions for Bitcoiners

projects.name: Satscode
projects.description: SatsCode is a development initiative building practical Bitcoin and decentralized tools for African communities. Our focus is creating lightweight, accessible bots and micro-apps that make Bitcoin education, payments, data, music, and user interaction easier on platforms people already use Telegram, Discord, X, and Nostr.
projects.initiatives: SatsCode is building a suite of lightweight Bitcoin developer tools, bots, and micro-apps designed for African users. Our work includes Bitcoin education bots, LNbits-based payment bots, mining simulators, blockchain exploration tools, and Nostr-powered systems — including FreeWave, a decentralized music protocol that plays signed Nostr commands. Our focus is enabling developers, communities, and educators to easily onboard, teach, transact, and experiment with Bitcoin using simple, mobile-friendly tools.
projects.challenges: SatsCode faces challenges such as limited funding for development, unreliable internet in some regions, difficulty onboarding users who have low technical literacy, and limited access to hardware for testing Bitcoin and Nostr tools. Collaboration across different African countries can also be tough due to distance and inconsistent infrastructure.

projects.name: Bitcoin Study Hubs
projects.description:
This work is about laying a strong, durable foundation for decentralized, physical Bitcoin learning spaces:

* Physical, decentralized Bitcoin learning spaces
    
* Bitcoin — no shitcoinery
    
* Local-language education
    
* Practical Bitcoin usage (Machankura, offline tools, mesh networks)
    
* Onboarding families and merchants
    
*  Training facilitators, not influencers
    
*  Building village-level circular economies
    
This is Bitcoin as money, education, and sovereignty.

projects.name: ZazaPay lightning
projects.description:
ZazaPay is a fintech payment platform that lets people send Bitcoin and the receiver gets local money instantly (like Mobile Money or bank transfer).
In simple terms .

It converts bitcoin → real everyday money automatically.

What it does


You send Bitcoin


ZazaPay converts it in real-time


The recipient receives GHS / KES / NGN directly to Mobile Money or bank


Settlement happens in seconds using the Lightning Network

projects.name: Bitzed
projects.description:
Bitzed is a non-KYC Bitcoin app in Zambia that lets users sell and buy Bitcoin directly into a wallet they control using mobile money, starting from as little as K1 (~60 sats). Transactions are instant and low-cost, powered by the Lightning Network.

Key Features:
• Lightning Network payments
• Ultra-low transaction fees
• Simple, user-friendly interface
projects.impact: Making Bitcoin easier to access and use through mobile money, Bitzed helps ordinary Zambians gain more control over how they save and transact, without heavy barriers or restrictions.

projects.name: Open Study Hubs
projects.description:
WHAT OPEN STUDY HUBS ARE

(https://geyser.fund/project/bitcoinstudyhubs)
*  Physical, decentralized Bitcoin learning spaces, modeled after the MyFirstBitcoin 
    
*  Bitcoin and Freedom — no shitcoinery
    
*  Local-language education
    
*  Practical Bitcoin usage (powered by Machankura)
    
*  Onboarding families and merchants
    
*  Training facilitators, not influencers
    
*   Building village-level circular economies
    

This is Bitcoin as money, education, and sovereignty.

HOW THIS SCALES (Decentralized By Design, Just The Same Way MyFirstBitcoin Is)

We begin with one hub.

The first phase prioritizes training facilitators instead of building centralized control.

Once facilitators are trained:

*   Communities operate their own hubs
    
*   Materials are localized
    
*   Guidance remains available without dependency
    
*   Growth happens horizontally, not top-down
    

This ensures genuine ownership and long-term sustainability.
projects.initiatives:
- Education programs (beginning with the first hub - the Genesis Hub, then expanding to other community-run study Hubs)

- Community building (when people understand Bitcoin, it's simple to use it in their daily activities)
projects.impact:
Prior to launching this project on [Geyser Fund](https://geyser.fund/project/bitcoinstudyhubs), I asked Kgothatso of Machankura, if I could assist with the Chichewa translation of the Machankura Wallet. He was grateful for the idea, so he gave me a go ahead. I helped with the Wallet menu translation, and Chichewa version is available on Machankura Wallet for those who may not understand English. I believe if people are taught about Bitcoin in their local language, it becomes more practical. Likewise, I'm working on translation of different Bitcoin beginners books and articles.

PHASE 1. 
Establish the first fully operational Bitcoin Study Hub, the Genesis Hub, and train facilitators.

PHASE 2. 
Expanding to other areas, in order to have various community-run study hubs. This ensures decentralized approach, similar to MyFirstBitcoin's.

PHASE 3.
Building circular economies. When people understand Bitcoin, they naturally begin to use it as a tool for community empowerment and individual sovereignty.
projects.challenges:
Open Study Hubs project is currently struggling to get the necessary resources to get fully established. These are meant to be physical spaces, where people (children, the youth, adults and elderly) come at their convenient time to learn about Bitcoin (freedom and responsibility). 
- We need a site for the learning space 
- We need teaching and learning materials (books, tools, demo kits, etc)

Build a chill Bitcoin-powered community in Ponta do Ouro where locals can use Bitcoin easily, keep money circulating right here on the beach, stay financially free, protect the ocean, and just live better together.

projects.name: BitDevs Kampala
projects.description:
BitDevs Kampala is Kampala's Bitcoin developers community. It is local chapter based in Kampala, Uganda, part of a broader global network of technically focused Bitcoin meetup groups.

It follows the BitDevs format: monthly meetups that are Bitcoin-only, technically focused, non-commercial, and privacy-respecting, using a Socratic discussion style that encourages participants to think critically and engage meaningfully regardless of their experience level.

The community is supported by Btrust, which provides administration, communication, and funding assistance. It serves as a local hub for developers, researchers, and technically curious individuals in Uganda to deepen their understanding of Bitcoin and has helped members gain access to broader opportunities in Bitcoin open-source development.

projects.name: BitJR Academy and Space
projects.description:
BitJR Academy and Space is a kid's Bitcoin community, founded in October 2024, focuses on teaching children in Livingstone, Zambia about money and Bitcoin from an early age through practical, engaging experiences. Learning is delivered through games, classes, meetups, competitions, and project fairs, alongside field trips where children can spend Bitcoin using the Lightning Network.

The initiative goes beyond theory by introducing real ownership and responsibility through tools like NFC cards and simple wallets like Blink. Children are not only learning about Bitcoin but actively using it in their daily lives.

The project is building a small kids-led Bitcoin circular economy by working with local merchants and local schools to enable everyday Lightning transactions. Connected to Bitcoin Victoria Falls and the global My First Bitcoin network, this work demonstrates how early Bitcoin education can drive grassroots adoption.

By empowering children to earn, use, and understand Bitcoin, the initiative creates a ripple effect where knowledge spreads into homes and communities, supporting long-term adoption.
projects.initiatives:
- Bitcoin education for children (ages 6–17) 
- Community meetups, competitions, and project fairs
- Building a kids-led circular economy (earn, spend, and save sats)
- Merchant onboarding to support everyday Bitcoin transactions (Local school tuckshops and community merchants)
projects.impact:
- Introduced Bitcoin education to children in Livingstone since October 2024
- Enabled more than 100 children to earn and spend sats
- Established early-stage kids Bitcoin circular economy with active usage
- Established Area Satoshi Club in local schools
- Taught 18 local school teachers about Bitcoin for effective communication
- Onboarded local school tuckshops
- Increased community awareness through meetups and merchant participation
- Connected local initiative to global networks like My First Bitcoin and CBAF
- Demonstrated that children can influence household and community adoption
projects.challenges:
- Limited access to NFC Bolt Cards for consistent participation (Most Kids do not have mobile devices)
- Onboarding and retaining merchants in a small local economy
- We do not have spacious and permanent learning hub
- Sustaining funding for activities, rewards, and expansion

projects.name: BitDevs Malawi
projects.description:
BitDevs Malawi is a grassroots Bitcoin education and developer community committed to growing technical understanding, practical skills, and open-source participation in Malawi’s Bitcoin ecosystem. We bring together students, developers, builders, and curious learners through meetups and university programs designed to make Bitcoin more accessible, more understandable, and more useful in real-world contexts.

Vision:

To build a financially empowered and technically skilled Malawi where individuals and communities can understand, build, and contribute to Bitcoin and open-source infrastructure.

Mission:  

To educate and equip Malawians with practical Bitcoin knowledge, foster a strong local developer community, and create learning spaces that encourage collaboration, experimentation, and contribution to the global Bitcoin ecosystem.

What we do:
  
- Run Bitcoin-focused meetups and technical discussions.  
- Host hands-on Bitcoin development and tooling.  
- Organize university outreach and training programs to introduce students to Bitcoin, open source, and protocol development.  
- Support community members in learning, building, and contributing to Bitcoin-related projects.  
- Create a bridge between local talent in Malawi and the global Bitcoin developer community.

Our focus: 

BitDevs Malawi is centered on education, community building, and technical growth. We aim to nurture a generation of Bitcoin builders who can participate meaningfully in open-source development while also helping expand Bitcoin knowledge across universities and local communities.
projects.initiatives:
Core Values and Principles
1. Bitcoin-only focus
2. Technical depth and curiosity 
3. Open-source collaboration 
4. Neutral and non-commercial space
5. Privacy and respect
6. Local leadership, global connection
7. Inclusion and accessibility
projects.impact:
Key achievements include:
- Running community learning sessions focused on Bitcoin development and tooling.
- Introducing students and developers to Bitcoin open-source collaboration.
- Building relationships with university communities and student leaders.
- Creating momentum around BitDevs-style technical meetups in Malawi.
- Helping connect local interest in Bitcoin with practical learning opportunities and real projects.
projects.challenges:
Like many grassroots communities, BitDevs Malawi faces a few important challenges as it grows. The biggest challenge is limited funding for consistent meetups, training materials, venue costs, internet access, and travel for outreach activities across universities and communities.

Another challenge is the limited number of local Bitcoin developers and mentors available to support new learners at scale. We also face the broader issue of low awareness of Bitcoin development opportunities in Malawi, which means we often need to spend extra time on education before contributors can begin working on technical projects.

Additional challenges include:
- Limited access to reliable infrastructure for in-person and hybrid sessions.
- Difficulty sustaining regular programs without dedicated financial support.
- The need for more structured pathways from learning to contribution.
- Time and resource constraints for volunteer organizers.

Despite these challenges, we remain committed to growing a strong, practical, and sustainable Bitcoin learning community in Malawi.

projects.name: Grimm App
projects.description:
Your bank, in your hands.

Grimm App is an open-source, self-custodial Bitcoin wallet built to make Bitcoin simple, accessible, and secure for everyone.

Today, using Bitcoin is still too complicated for most people especially in Africa. Many users face overly technical interfaces, dependence on centralized platforms that can freeze funds, limited control over their money, and experiences that are not designed for their realities.

Grimm App solves these problems by providing a simple and intuitive solution that allows anyone to:

Fully own and control their funds through self-custody
Send and receive Bitcoin both on-chain and via the Lightning Network
Use Bitcoin without requiring advanced technical knowledge

Whether you are a beginner or an experienced user, Grimm App gives you the freedom to use Bitcoin with a smooth, secure, and user-friendly experience.
projects.challenges:
Grimm App is currently facing challenges mainly around growth, community engagement, and ecosystem development:

Limited open-source contributions, as it remains difficult to attract and retain active contributors
Increasing visibility and adoption through participation in events and consistent promotion of the app
Organizing educational initiatives around Bitcoin, particularly within French-speaking communities where access to quality resources is still limited
Building a strong and engaged community to support long-term adoption and collaboration

projects.name: Rurbit
projects.description:
Rurbit is designed to bring financial sovereignty to rural and remote communities where traditional banking infrastructure is limited, unreliable, or entirely absent. Many rural regions face unstable internet connectivity, long distances to bank branches, high remittance fees, and exclusion from digital financial services. Rurbit changes that!

Built on the Bitcoin Lightning Network, Rurbit enables instant, low-fee, peer-to-peer payments that work even in low-connectivity or no-internet environments. Through USSD on feature phone or smartphones.
Rurbit is not just a payment tool. It is infrastructure for a circular economy, where farmers, artisans, shopkeepers, and neighbors trade directly with one another, keeping wealth circulating locally instead of losing it to intermediaries, payment processors, and distant financial institutions.

By combining Bitcoin’s global liquidity with Lightning’s instant settlement and rural-first design principles, Rurbit empowers communities to:

Accept and send payments instantly
Operate in low-bandwidth or offline scenarios
Eliminate excessive transaction fees
Reduce reliance on centralized banking systems
Build resilient, self-sustaining local economies

With Rurbit, even a basic feature phone can become a gateway to the global Bitcoin network.

Our mission is to empower rural and underserved communities with accessible, low-cost, and resilient Bitcoin payment infrastructure that enables true financial sovereignty.
Our vision is a future where you can arrive in Burundi with nothing but a Bitcoin wallet and live entirely off your sats.

Ten years from now, we'll see in Burundi: Farmers in open-air markets accept sats for their harvest as naturally as they do cash, cross-border traders settle transactions instantly across mountains without the risk of physical cash transport and youth freelancers in Burundi earn global wages without ever leaving their homes.

Rurbit is the bridge to this future ensuring that the magic of Bitcoin reaches the last mile, so that every village can stand independent, connected, and economically sovereign.
projects.initiatives:
• Rural Community building
• Remittance channel
• Merchants onboarding
• Rural Bitcoiners
• Circular Economy
• Bitcoin Adoption
projects.impact: As we just started, Rurbit’s biggest achievement right now is turning Bitcoin from a distant global technology into a practical tool for rural freedom. It is laying the groundwork for a future where people in villages can accept sats, spend sats, earn globally, trade locally, and participate confidently in the Bitcoin economy, even without reliable internet.
projects.challenges:
•The "USSD Tax": Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) often charge high fees for access to USSD codes. Navigating the cost-per-session model while trying to keep Bitcoin transaction fees low for farmers is a difficult balancing act.
• Central Bank Compliance: The BRB (Bank of the Republic of Burundi) is still defining how to handle "Virtual Asset Service Providers" (VASPs). Operating a USSD service that touches both the telecom network and Bitcoin puts RuBit in a "gray zone" that could lead to sudden licensing requirements or shutdowns.

projects.name: Bitcoin Students Network
projects.description: The Bitcoin Students Network (BSN) is a global network of students, professors, and university Bitcoin clubs working to accelerate Bitcoin education, talent development, and collaboration across campuses. BSN supports the next generation of Bitcoin builders by connecting students to resources, mentorship, industry opportunities, events, and one another.

projects.name: BitmernSolo
projects.description:
BitmernSolo is an independent Bitcoin mining initiative dedicated to empowering African miners through open‑source innovation, trusted vendor support, and streamlined solo‑mining solutions. By combining technical expertise with community‑driven standards, BitmernSolo helps miners maximize efficiency, maintain sovereignty, and contribute to the decentralization of the Bitcoin network.

Mission
Our mission is to make Bitcoin mining accessible, transparent, and sustainable for individuals and small businesses across Africa. We aim to provide miners with reliable tools, accurate branding resources, and vendor pathways that strengthen trust within the global Bitcoin ecosystem.

Vision
We envision a thriving African Bitcoin mining community where miners operate independently, supported by open‑source technologies and verified vendor networks. BitmernSolo seeks to position Africa as a key contributor to Bitcoin’s resilience, ensuring that miners on the continent can participate fully in the global economy while maintaining control over their operations.
projects.impact:
Impact & Achievements
Verified Vendor Pathway: Successfully submitted vendor entry and branding assets for review in the Bitaxe open‑source ecosystem, positioning BitmernSolo as a trusted African vendor.

Open‑Source Contribution: Actively engaged in GitHub repository management, ensuring transparent workflows and compliance with community standards.

Brand Identity Established: Developed and published a consistent logo and vendor profile, strengthening recognition and credibility in the bitcoin mining space.

Community Engagement: Supported African miners with clear documentation, step‑by‑step onboarding guidance, and alignment with open‑source best practices.

Regional Representation: Advanced visibility of African mining initiatives by integrating BitmernSolo into global Bitcoin vendor networks.
projects.challenges:
Brand Visibility: Establishing stronger recognition within the African Bitcoin mining community and global open‑source ecosystem.

Resource Constraints: Limited access to funding and infrastructure for scaling solo‑mining operations across diverse regions.

Community Outreach: Expanding educational efforts to onboard new miners and ensure adoption of open‑source standards.

Technical Integration: Ensuring compatibility of mining tools with evolving Bitcoin protocols and hardware updates.

projects.name: Bityara
projects.description:
BitYara (Bitcoin Yara) is a child-focused Bitcoin education initiative dedicated to introducing children and young people across Nigeria to Bitcoin, financial literacy, and digital innovation through creative, engaging, and culturally relevant learning experiences.
We believe that understanding money, value, savings, and emerging technologies should begin early. Through storytelling, art, games, school outreach programs, workshops, and community activities, BitYara makes Bitcoin education accessible and enjoyable for young learners.

Our mission is to equip the next generation with the knowledge and skills needed to navigate an increasingly digital world while fostering critical thinking, creativity, and financial responsibility.

Our vision is to build a future where every African child has the opportunity to understand Bitcoin and participate confidently in the evolving global economy.
projects.initiatives:
•Education Programs 
•School outreach 
•Community Engagement
projects.impact: Since its launch, BitYara has taken meaningful steps toward advancing youth Bitcoin education. A key milestone was the successful organizing of a Kids Bitcoin Pizza Day event, where children learned about Bitcoin, money, and the history of the Bitcoin pizza day through storytelling, creativity activities, and interactive discussions. BitYara is also  actively preparing for school outreach programs designed to introduce students to Bitcoin, financial literacy, and digital innovation in engaging and age-appropriate ways.
projects.challenges: As a growing initiative, BitYara faces challenges related to mostly to  funding, and the development of high-quality learning resources for children. We are also working to increase awareness and understanding of Bitcoin education among parents, educators, and communities while building sustainable structures that will allow us to scale our impact across Nigeria.

projects.name: HakiFedha | Bitcoin & Privacy for Civil Society in Africa
projects.description:
HakiFedha takes its name from two Swahili words: Haki, meaning justice, rights, and fairness, and Fedha, meaning money and finance. The name reflects a core conviction: financial sovereignty and privacy are not technical luxuries reserved for the wealthy or the technically skilled. They are fundamental rights, and in many parts of Africa, they are under direct threat.

Across the continent, civil society actors face increasing financial and digital pressure. Bank accounts are frozen without court orders. Cross-border transfers are blocked. Donor funds are restricted at the point of entry. Governments use financial infrastructure as a tool of political control, dissolving dissent not through arrests but through strangulation. HakiFedha exists to help civil society build the resilience to survive and operate in exactly these conditions.

The platform provides non-technical, experience-based guidance on Bitcoin, the Lightning Network, self-custody, privacy and anti-censorship platforms, and the operational security practices that protect people working in high-pressure environments. All content is written for non-technical users and grounded in African realities, with resources available in English and Swahili, and additional languages in development.

Alongside the knowledge hub, HakiFedha operates BitMentor, a peer-to-peer volunteer network connecting experienced Bitcoiners with civil society groups who need hands-on guidance. BitMentor supports organisations with self-custody setups, multisig treasury management, Bitcoin accounting and compliance, BTCPay Server configuration, Bitcoin payroll, and how to communicate Bitcoin adoption clearly to donors and boards. 

Looking ahead, HakiFedha plans to use Nostr for BitMentor mentor verification and reputation, building trust infrastructure in a decentralised way.

HakiFedha also maintains the Africa Financial Tyranny Index (AFTI), an open dataset tracking documented incidents in which African governments have used financial mechanisms to restrict, surveil, weaken, or dissolve civil society organisations and human rights defenders.

Mission
To equip civil society actors across Africa with the knowledge, tools, and human support they need to achieve financial sovereignty, protect their communications, and continue their work regardless of the financial and digital pressures they face.

Vision
A continent where every human rights defender, journalist, whistleblower, and community organiser has access to the financial tools and privacy knowledge they need to operate freely, safely, and with dignity, and where financial access can never again be weaponised to silence those who speak truth to power.
projects.initiatives:
1. HakiFedha Knowledge Hub — Multilingual, non-technical guides on Bitcoin, financial sovereignty, privacy tools, and operational security, built specifically for African civil society contexts
2. BitMentor Network — Peer-to-peer volunteer mentorship connecting experienced Bitcoiners with NGOs, activists, and defenders needing hands-on guidance
3. Faragha Assistant — An embedded guidance tool helping users navigate the hub in real time, bridging static documentation and human mentorship
4. Africa Financial Tyranny Index (AFTI) — An open dataset documenting incidents where African governments have used financial mechanisms to suppress civil society
5. Templates and Toolkits — Free, practical resources for NGO Bitcoin governance, treasury management, accounting, compliance, and crisis preparedness

projects.name: OSGuild
projects.description: OSGuild is a structured pathway for new developers to enter the Bitcoin open-source ecosystem. It provides a guided environment where contributors learn by working on forked, real-world projects, earning feedback and recognition for quality contributions. In short, OSGuild aims to turn beginners into trusted open-source developers.
projects.initiatives:
-Pathways 
-Open source community in africa

projects.name: BearBull
projects.description: BearBull is a Lightning-native Bitcoin price prediction platform. Users stake sats on BTC's short-term direction — Bear (down) or Bull (up) — and winners split the pool instantly via the Lightning Network. Fully non-custodial: funds never leave the user's wallet, and settlement is peer-to-peer.
